reasoning: The informal statement explicitly says 'd(p, q) > δ' (strict inequality). The corrected formalization uses `dist p q > δ`, matching exactly. The original uses `dist p q ≥ δ` (non-strict), which is a weaker conclusion — it's easier to prove ≥ than >. While the full existential statements are logically equivalent (you can always shrink δ by half), the original does not literally match what the informal asks for. The original is weaker (non-strict where strict was requested).
